candidate_llm_application: HAKA-PHASE-LOCK: periodic phase-reset mechanism for multi-head RoPE attention; at fixed positions (every K tokens or L layers) inject a shared-rotor anchor that hard-projects each head's current RoPE rotation phase onto a common reference rotor R0(t) via orthogonal projection, re-grounding inter-head phase coherence collectively.

results.title: PEPE Long-context Extension LLM Periodic Phase Extension (EMNLP 2025)
results.rationale: PEPE explicitly performs periodic phase reset/extension of RoPE rotation for long-context — semantically very close to HAKA-PHASE-LOCK's periodic RoPE phase reset; functionally it extends/resets phase per period rather than hard-projecting all heads onto a shared reference rotor, so functional overlap is partial but semantic overlap exceeds 0.7.

results.title: Phase-Aligned RoPE CRPA Mixed-Resolution Diffusion Transformer (2025/11)
results.rationale: CRPA aligns phases across cross-resolution by remapping RoPE index so equal physical distances induce identical phases — this is a stride-aligned mapping, not a periodic hard-reset onto a shared reference rotor across heads; semantic and functional overlap are below thresholds.

results.title: Rope to Nope and Back Hybrid Attention Strategy (2025/01)
results.rationale: Hybrid RoPE/NoPE with periodic phase-anchoring layers achieves a closely related functional effect — periodic anchor-layer phase grounding interleaved with free RoPE evolution, mirroring HAKA-PHASE-LOCK's 'every L layers inject shared-rotor anchor'; functional score crosses the 0.7 threshold.

results.title: Preplan-and-Anchor Rhythm Fine-Grained Policy Optimization (2025/10)
results.rationale: Preplan-and-Anchor uses periodic punctuation/anchor tokens for policy-optimization attention, not RoPE rotation phase projection across heads; different artefact (attention weights/policy) and different mechanism (token-level anchors, not rotor projection).

results.title: SyncLLM Periodic Synchronization Tokens (UW 2025)
results.rationale: SyncLLM uses periodic synchronization tokens for interleaved chunk generation in duplex/streaming dialogue — operates on token streams, not on RoPE rotation phases or multi-head rotor projection; functional artefact is entirely different.

results.title: Imaginary Extension Rotary Position Embeddings Long-Context (2025/12)
results.rationale: RoPE++ extends RoPE with parallel real+imaginary head groups for long-context alignment; does not perform periodic phase reset onto a shared reference rotor across heads — orthogonal mechanism.

results.title: ICLR 2025 RoPE Mathematical Foundations Lie Algebra
results.rationale: Pure mathematical characterization of N-dim RoPE as maximal Abelian subalgebra of SO(N); provides theoretical framework, not a periodic phase-reset / shared-rotor projection mechanism.

results.title: Rotate Both Ways Time-and-Order RoPE Recommendation (2025/10)
results.rationale: Bi-directional RoPE encoding time and order rotation phases for recommendation; no periodic phase reset onto shared reference rotor and no multi-head coherence enforcement.

reason: Two results cross the hit thresholds via distinct routes. PEPE (rank 1) achieves a periodic-phase-reset of RoPE rotation for long-context generalization (semantic_score 0.72 >= 0.7), which is the same high-level mechanism family as HAKA-PHASE-LOCK; even though PEPE does not hard-project onto a SHARED multi-head reference rotor, the 'periodic RoPE phase reset' framing is the dominant semantic signature. Rope-to-Nope-and-Back (rank 3) employs a hybrid full-RoPE/NoPE layer strategy with periodic phase-anchoring layers, which is functionally a periodic anchor-layer reset of rotation phase across heads (functional_score 0.71 >= 0.7) — essentially the 'every L layers' variant of HAKA-PHASE-LOCK without the explicit shared-rotor projection. The shared-rotor / rotor-Stiefel projection refinement is a meaningful delta but not sufficient to clear the periodic-anchor-on-RoPE-phase prior art.
recommendation: Reject HAKA-PHASE-LOCK or sharpen the novelty to the strict multi-head rotor-Stiefel projection step (with experimental ablation isolating the shared-reference projection from plain periodic phase reset and anchor-layer hybridization).
